title: ZIA Web Policy Violation Trending
spl:
index=proxy sourcetype="zscaler:web" action=BLOCKED
| timechart span=1h count by rule_name
| where count>0
description: Web policy trending shows how often controls block risky or non-compliant traffic.
value: Web policy trending shows how often controls block risky or non-compliant traffic.
implementation: Ingest ZIA NSS or log streaming. Normalize rule and URL category. Tune noisy categories.
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Web.Web by Web.status, Web.http_method, Web.dest span=1h | sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Legitimate use of dual-purpose administrative tools (e.g. PowerShell, SSH, cloud CLIs) during maintenance or deployment can match this pattern — correlate with change tickets, asset criticality and user role before escalating.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_zscaler`.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype=zscaler:web`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Ingest ZIA NSS or log streaming. Normalize rule and URL category. Tune noisy categories.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=proxy sourcetype="zscaler:web" action=BLOCKED
| timechart span=1h count by rule_name
| where count>0
```

Understanding this SPL

**ZIA Web Policy Violation Trending** — Web policy trending shows how often controls block risky or non-compliant traffic.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=zscaler:web`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_zscaler`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: proxy; **sourcetype**: zscaler:web. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Detection type** for this use case: TTP — interpret thresholds and fields in that context.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=proxy, sourcetype="zscaler:web". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `timechart` plots the metric over time using **span=1h** buckets with a separate series **by rule_name** — ideal for trending and alerting on this use case.
• Filters the current rows with `where count>0` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Web.Web by Web.status, Web.http_method, Web.dest span=1h | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**ZIA Web Policy Violation Trending** — Web policy trending shows how often controls block risky or non-compliant traffic.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype=zscaler:web`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_zscaler`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Detection type** for this use case: TTP — interpret thresholds and fields in that context.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Web.Web` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (blocks), Bar chart (rules).
grandmaExplanation: Web policy trending shows how often controls block risky or non-compliant traffic — so we know when protection is off, out of date, or bypassed.
